issues.title: [Epic · Week 6] Public preview and community readiness
issues.outcome: Complete the public preview and community readiness outcome without pulling later platform work into the current gate.
scope: Close the phase only with evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Every child issue has a clear owner, milestone, and acceptance criteria.
Blocked work is represented using issue dependencies.
The phase gate has explicit evidence.
definitionOfDone: Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Week 6 · QA] Web MVP acceptance, regression, content, and release-readiness gate
issues.outcome: Verify the complete wiki MVP and produce a transparent readiness report before public preview.
scope: Keyboard, contrast, responsive, offline, SEO, performance, security, and legal checks.
acceptanceCriteria: Performance and accessibility exceptions are documented and approved.
The readiness report links reproducible evidence.
definitionOfDone: Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Week 6 · Release] GitHub Pages preview, release checklist, rollback, and operations notes
issues.outcome: Publish a reproducible GitHub Pages preview with documented release and rollback procedures.
scope: Build and deploy with least-privilege Actions.
Add release checklist, smoke checks, and rollback instructions.
Document future hosting evaluation using GitHub Student resources.
acceptanceCriteria: Deployment is reproducible from the default branch or an approved release workflow.
No write permission exceeds the job requirement.
Direct links and assets work under the repository base path.
Rollback procedure is tested or dry-run evidenced.
definitionOfDone: Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Week 6 · Community] Contribution guide, issue forms, content review, and maintainer boundaries
issues.outcome: Invite useful community contributions while protecting quality, legal boundaries, and the single maintainer's capacity.
scope: Bug, data correction, feature, accessibility, and asset-permission issue forms.
Code of conduct and maintainer response expectations.
AI-assisted contribution disclosure policy focused on reviewable evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Contributors can propose a correction without understanding the whole codebase.
Issue forms request reproduction/source details without private data.
Maintainer boundaries and response expectations are honest.
definitionOfDone: Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Week 6 · Release] Final catalog accuracy, source, asset, and editorial audit
issues.outcome: Review the exact catalog and media artifacts included in the public preview.
scope: Check source links, game-version labels, uncertain values, prose originality, permission evidence, alt text, and broken relationships.
acceptanceCriteria: Every published asset has an approved record.
Every high-impact fact has a source record.
No placeholder is presented as official content.
Audit report identifies reviewer, commit, catalog version, and exceptions.
definitionOfDone: Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.
